description_long: This object is a vintage-style biplane with a double set of straight wings, painted in a tricolor scheme of blue, white, and red. The fuselage is streamlined and tapers towards the tail, with a rounded nose housing a wooden propeller at the front. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with visible struts and support wires connecting the wings and landing gear. The tail section features a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, all painted to match the main color scheme. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, giving it a classic taildragger configuration.
description: A vintage biplane with blue, white, and red coloring, double straight wings, an open cockpit, and a wooden propeller.
description_view_0: Front view showing the double wings, open cockpit, and wooden propeller at the nose.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the stacked wings, open cockpit, and taildragger landing gear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section with single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the wing struts, cockpit placement, and tail skid.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two sets of straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
